response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with strong web, testing, and craftsmanship credentials, but the role requires deep hardware-adjacent expertise: SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. Aside from low-latency DLang services at Dunnhumby and brief C/C++ work at National Instruments, there is no evidence of chip-level modeling, deep-learning workload optimization, or SIMD/MIMD architecture experience. The profile does not match the core requirements of a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience meeting the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware performance analysis experience
No deep neural network training or inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product full-stack, not principal-level hardware/performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
Systems-level performance work in low-latency services
